claim: Every task delegation to an agent shall be recorded as a versioned file under delegations/ (DG-<n>.md, created and closed only via a dedicated delegate tool), so git carries the who/when/what history of every hand-off.
why: Resolver verdict (2026-07-05, verbatim): 'давай делегировать все задачи через файлы, и вести их историю в гите'. REPLACES the graph-node Delegation design (R-domain-delegation-as-node, R-domain-delegation-persists): the resolver chose file-based delegations over a new graph node type -- git history on committed files IS the audit trail, making a dedicated Delegation entity unnecessary. Not yet ported in the Go port: there is no delegate command registered in the Go CLI (cmd/hotam) nor in the tools registry (internal/methodology/tools_data.go), and no delegations/ directory is scaffolded or read by any Go code. The historical Python mechanism (tools/delegate.py creating DG-<n>.md with create/close lifecycle, enforced by test_tool_delegate.py) is the reference for a future port. When ported, the Go version would register a delegate command, write DG-<n>.md files under a delegations/ path, and record hand-off history. Bookkeeping note (etap C, lens-5-quality #3, 2026-07-10): in the Python era this requirement was flipped STRUCTURAL -> ENFORCED because the enforcer (test_tool_delegate.py) existed; in the Go port that enforcer does not exist, so the requirement stands at PROSE pending the port.
